prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training - maximize carbohydrate loading and sustained energy for long-distance running

solution_criteria: Meal plan contains 4-5 meals/snacks distributed throughout the day
Total daily calories align with marathon training needs (approximately 3000-3500 kcal)
Carbohydrates comprise 60-65% of total calories with emphasis on complex carbs
Includes protein for muscle recovery and hydration/electrolyte considerations
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a high-endurance athlete (marathon runner) requiring maximum caloric intake and carbohydrate loading, emphasizing sustained energy and recovery nutrition

prompt_inputs.goal: Maximize muscle recovery and strength gains with high protein intake for weightlifting training

solution_criteria: Meal plan spans exactly 1 day with 4-5 meals
Daily protein intake meets or exceeds 2.0g per kg of body weight (170g minimum)
Includes pre-workout meal (1-2 hours before) and post-workout meal (within 1 hour after)
Compact format with practical, realistic foods and portion sizes
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a strength/power athlete (weightlifter) with specific macro requirements (high protein), including pre/post-workout meal timing and muscle recovery priorities

prompt_inputs.goal: High-performance fuel for soccer match and recovery

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks for one full day
All meals and snacks are vegan-compliant with no animal products
Provides adequate protein and carbohydrates to support soccer performance and recovery
Plan is compact and concise (minimal elaboration beyond meal descriptions)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete
scenario: Testing with a sport-specific dietary constraint (vegan soccer player) that requires the meal plan to accommodate ethical restrictions while meeting athletic performance and energy demands
